title: Bonamy et al. Conjecture 33
statement_text: For every graph $H$, the function $p(s, H)$ appearing in the Kühn–Osthus theorem (that every $K_{s,s}$-free graph $G$ with average degree at least $p(s, H)$ contains an induced subdivision of $H$) can be taken to be a polynomial in $s$.
context_text: Kühn and Osthus proved that for every graph $H$ and integer $s$ there is an integer $p(s,H)$ such that every $K_{s,s}$-free graph of average degree at least $p(s,H)$ contains an induced subdivision of $H$, but their bounds are roughly triply exponential in $s$ for fixed $H$. Bonamy, Bousquet, Pilipczuk, Rzążewski, Thomassé, and Walczak conjectured (as Conjecture 33 of their paper) that a polynomial bound suffices.
attributed_to: Bonamy, Bousquet, Pilipczuk, Rzążewski, Thomassé, and Walczak
paper_contribution: Proves (Corollary 1.6) that a singly exponential bound suffices: there exists a constant $C$ so that every $K_{s,s}$-free graph with average degree at least $k^{Cs^3}$ contains an induced subdivision of $K_k$, improving the triply exponential bound of Kühn–Osthus.
notes: Cited as [2, Conjecture 33] in the paper. Polynomial bound remains open; the paper closes the gap from triply exponential to singly exponential.
paper_title: Induced $C_4$-free subgraphs with large average degree

title: Polynomial degree-bounding function for hereditary degree-bounded classes
statement_text: Is there always a polynomial degree-bounding function for every hereditary degree-bounded class of graphs? That is, for every hereditary degree-bounded class $\mathcal{F}$, does there exist a polynomial $g$ such that $d(G) \leq g(\tau(G))$ for every $G \in \mathcal{F}$?
context_text: Corollary 1.4 establishes that every hereditary degree-bounded class admits an exponential degree-bounding function of the form $C^{s^3}$, which is the first bound of any type on the rate of growth. The paper notes explicitly in both the abstract and the introduction that it is open whether a polynomial always suffices. The analogous question for $\chi$-boundedness has a negative answer (Briański, Davies, and Walczak).
notes: Stated in prose in the abstract and in the introduction without a labelled theorem environment.
paper_title: Induced $C_4$-free subgraphs with large average degree

title: Polynomial bound in k for C4-free subgraphs
statement_text: Does there exist a polynomial $p(k)$ such that every graph with average degree at least $p(k)$ contains a $C_4$-free subgraph with average degree at least $k$?
context_text: Montgomery, Pokrovskiy, and Sudakov proved that every graph with average degree at least $k^{Ck^2}$ contains a $C_4$-free subgraph of average degree at least $k$, and also gave a lower bound of $k^{3-o(1)}$. The paper describes determining whether a polynomial in $k$ suffices as 'a very interesting problem'.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction without a labelled theorem environment; the gap between the lower bound $k^{3-o(1)}$ and the upper bound $k^{Ck^2}$ makes this a natural open problem.
paper_title: Induced $C_4$-free subgraphs with large average degree
